stories.title: HMRC Manuals Weekly Roundup: 6 July 2026
stories.summary: HMRC published its weekly roundup of manual updates covering corporation tax and other tax matters, providing the latest guidance for practitioners and taxpayers.

stories.title: VAT Changes Under HMRC's 2026 Transformation Roadmap
stories.summary: HMRC is introducing an improved VAT repayments tracker as part of its 2026 Transformation Roadmap, with initial functionality expected to be available to customers during 2026–2027.

stories.title: HMRC Collected £865.2 Billion in 2024/25 Tax Year
stories.summary: HMRC collected £865.2 billion during the 2024/25 tax year, representing 93.6% of all tax due. Small businesses account for 62% of the total tax gap.

stories.title: Jamaican Man Who Lived in UK for 26 Years Facing Deportation Under New Immigration Bill
stories.summary: A Jamaican national who has lived more than half his life in the UK is facing deportation in one of the first cases under the new anti-immigration legislation, highlighting the real-world impact of the government's stricter immigration policies.

stories.title: Foreign Secretary Chatham House Essay: Britain's Place in the New World Order
stories.summary: Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper published an essay outlining how Britain can build global alliances amid geopolitical instability, economic coercion, AI challenges, and climate change to secure the future of its people.

stories.title: Views Sought to Transform Benefit Paid to Carers
stories.summary: The DWP is consulting on reforms to the carer's benefit, having already launched a reassessment exercise reviewing 200,000 cases — with around 25,000 set to see debts reduced, cancelled, or refunded.

stories.title: Skills England Annual Skills Report 2026 Links Training to Employment
stories.summary: The 2026 Skills England report highlights the integration of skills, apprenticeships, and adult further education into DWP, aiming to better link training with employment opportunities.

agencies.name: DEFRA
agencies.description: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

stories.title: Marine Recovery Fund: Terms and Conditions for Offshore Wind Developers Published
stories.summary: DEFRA published the Marine Recovery Fund (MRF) terms and conditions for offshore wind developers, setting out requirements for marine environmental mitigation and recovery.

stories.title: Shropshire Researchers Track Slug Damage to Help Arable Farmers
stories.summary: Arable farmers may soon be able to better manage slug infestations thanks to new research by a Shropshire-based university working with volunteer 'slug sleuth' farmers to monitor crop damage.

stories.title: Government Responds to Consultation on Stronger Penalties for Water Company Offences
stories.summary: DEFRA published its summary of responses to the consultation on new penalties for water company offences, signalling the government's intent to strengthen enforcement against polluters.

stories.title: UK Fighter Jets Intercept Russian Plane Over Norwegian Sea
stories.summary: Royal Air Force fighter jets intercepted a Russian maritime patrol aircraft after it 'repeatedly approached' a UK carrier strike group in the Norwegian Sea, in a significant operational deployment.

stories.title: RAF Museum Framework Document Published
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence published the updated RAF Museum Framework Document, setting out the operating framework for the RAF Museum going forward.

stories.title: UK and Gulf Strike Historic Multi-Billion-Pound Trade Deal
stories.summary: The UK and Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) have struck a landmark trade deal, with the Department for Business and Trade promising significant boosts to wages and GDP through the agreement.

stories.title: Research Published on Behavioural Insights into Digital Services Trade
stories.summary: DBT commissioned and published research with UK businesses to understand how they trade services internationally, aimed at unlocking digital trade barriers.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: Businesses Across Britain Sign Up to Cyber Resilience Pledge
stories.summary: Major firms including M&S, Nationwide, and ITV have signed up to DSIT's Cyber Resilience Pledge, as ministers urge businesses to strengthen their cyber defences amid rising threats.

stories.title: Women in Tech Taskforce Consults on Building Inclusive Future Tech Sector
stories.summary: DSIT's Women in Tech Taskforce, launched in December 2025, is consulting on building a future tech sector that works for everyone, seeking views on inclusion and diversity in the technology industry.

stories.title: Consultant Doctors in England Vote for NHS Strikes Over Pay and Working Week
stories.summary: Consultant doctors in England have voted for strikes over the next 12 months in pursuit of higher pay and a shorter working week, prompting fears of renewed disruption to NHS services.

stories.title: NHS to Use AI on Its App to Direct Patients to Appropriate Services
stories.summary: The NHS is introducing AI on the NHS App to help direct patients to the most appropriate services, aiming to reduce waiting times and improve access to care.

stories.title: NHS to Rate English Trusts on Tackling Violence and Racism Towards Staff
stories.summary: NHS England is introducing a new rating system for English trusts to measure their performance in tackling violence and racism towards healthcare staff.

stories.title: July 2026 Postal Reforms: Universal Service Changes Now Underway
stories.summary: Royal Mail and the Communication Workers Union (CWU) reached agreement in April 2026 on deploying Universal Service Obligation (USO) changes, with revised service arrangements now rolling out across the UK from July 2026.

stories.title: Dog Attacks on Postal Workers Fall 8% to 2,019 Incidents
stories.summary: Royal Mail announced that dog attacks on its staff fell 8% to 2,019 incidents over the past year, down from 2,197 in 2024/25, though the number remains significant.
